mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar XJ 4.2 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Jaguar XJ 4.2 engine (XK6) typically costs £4,500–£7,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500. A full rebuild typically costs around £5,000. These are specialist-only replacements — the XK6 requires classic Jaguar engine expertise.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar XJ 4.2 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The XK6 4.2 is one of Jaguar's most legendary engines — famously durable and characterful. However, early blocks (pre-1983) are prone to cracking between bores. The Series 3 block (1983-on) has slotted water passages that prevent this. Tappet guides can loosen on higher-mileage examples. With proper maintenance, these engines are extremely robust.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar XJ 4.2?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pre-1983 block cracking (coolant loss, overheating, £4,000–£6,000), tappet guide loosening (tapping noise, £1,500–£3,500), cooling system wear (water pump, thermostat, £300–£800), and head gasket failure (£1,500–£3,000). The Series 3 (1983-on) is the most reliable version.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar XJ 4.2 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: XJ values range from £5,000–£20,000 depending on Series, condition and mileage. A £4,500–£7,000 reconditioned engine represents 20–80% of vehicle value — our 60% Jaguar scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained Series 3, replacement is often justified. For an ageing Series 1/2 with other issues, assess scrap value first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar XJ 4.2 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The XJ 4.2 uses the XK6 engine code — Jaguar's legendary 4.2-litre straight-six. The engine was used across Series 1 (1968–1973), Series 2 (1973–1979) and Series 3 (1979–1986) XJs. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the block cracking issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pre-1983 blocks can sometimes be repaired using a stitching process (£4,000–£6,000) — but in many cases, a replacement block or complete engine is more cost-effective (£4,500–£7,000). The later Series 3 block (1983-on) has slotted water passages that prevent the issue. At the first sign of coolant loss or overheating in a pre-1983 car, book an inspection immediately.

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current market ranges for the XK6 4.2 engine across all three Series. replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an XJ 4.2 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent Jaguar specialist) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). The straight-six is relatively accessible in the XJ's engine bay.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Jaguar XJ 4.2 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Stitching repair possible; often cheaper to replace the block
Head rebuild — viable on a well-maintained car
Replacement often £4,500–£7,000 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Jaguar XJ 4.2? Check These First
buyIf: * Series 3 (1983-on) with revised slotted-water-passage block — the most robust XK6 variant
* Documented engine rebuild history (head, bottom-end, cooling system)
* Full service history with regular oil changes (specialist maintained)
avoidIf: * Pre-1983 car with any evidence of coolant loss or overheating — block cracking risk
* Tapping/clicking from the top of the engine — tappet guide issue is serious
* Poor service history — the XK6 is robust but not invincible
* Rust — XJs are valuable; body condition is as important as the engine

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The XK6 4.2 is one of the greatest engines ever built — smooth, characterful and famously durable. The Series 3 (1983-on) with revised block is the most robust version. Tappet guides, cooling system components and head gaskets are the main concerns on higher-mileage examples. Buy the newest Series 3 you can afford, maintain the cooling system, and the XK6 will reward you.

cta.label: → Compare Jaguar XJ 4.2 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar XJ 4.2 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any tapping or clicking (tappet guide issue warning)
* Service History — Check the year to identify which Series — pre-1983 block cracking risk
* Coolant Level — Check for any loss — head gasket or block cracking risk
* Block Type — 1983-on has slotted water passages; pre-1983 is more prone to cracking
* Oil Condition — Any mayonnaise? (head gasket failure or oil contamination)
* Water Pump — Any whining or leaking? Cooling system components are common wear items
* Engine Performance — Test acceleration; any hesitation or rough running?
* Rust Check — XJs are prone to rust; body, sills and subframes are critical

engineCodes.matchNote: This table's rows match Section 1B's Era Map exactly.

items.label: * Fuel System
items.value: Triple SU carburettors (Series 1/early Series 2) / Lucas fuel injection (late Series 2/Series 3)
